# HK City Pulse (hkgov-rethink)

> Agentic monitoring that surfaces what the Hong Kong Government press room leaves
> unsaid. A Rust platform that ingests HKGOV open data (HKMA monetary stats,
> data.gov.hk, press releases), runs deterministic anomaly detection across
> sources, and flags where the official narrative and the published data disagree
> — with evidence you can verify. Detection is pure Rust and reproducible (same
> data in, same findings out); the LLM only frames results. No API key required
> for read-only data.

## What it finds

These are produced by the project's own detectors against live HKGOV open data.
Each finding is an `Insight` record with pointers back to the source rows.

- `series_jump` (critical): a value that moved sharply in one settlement window
  with no attributing press release. Example: HIBOR overnight +99.3% over a
  single window, unattributed by HKMA.
- `outlier`: a sustained regime that a single-day spike detector would miss,
  caught by a MAD robust-z scan. Example: a sub-1.3% HIBOR cluster across 8 days.
- `cross_source_gap`: dates where a press release was issued with no matching
  statistical data row (or vice versa) — the literal "press room leaves it
  untold" signal, with the specific dates as evidence.
- `silence_index` (`/v1/silence-index`): those signals rolled into a single,
  versioned, citable number — how much did HKMA not explain? Pass `?period=YYYY-Qn`
  to scope to one quarter.

## The determinism guarantee

Every number is reproducible. Same data in, same findings out. The agent layer
(insights, Q&A, alerting) works end to end with zero external dependencies and
no API key. The LLM only frames; detection is pure Rust.

## Key endpoints

All under the configured API prefix (default `/v1`) unless noted. Root paths
(`/health`, `/ready`, `/dashboard`, `/llms.txt`) are unauthenticated. Versioned
data routes may require an API key when the operator configures one
(`HKGOV_API__API_KEY`); omit the key in config for an open, key-less deployment.

- [GET /health](/health) — liveness probe (status + degraded flag).
- [GET /ready](/ready) — readiness probe; 503 when any source circuit is open or
  no dataset has warmed.
- [GET /v1/sources](/v1/sources) — list of ingested datasets with record counts.
- [GET /v1/categories](/v1/categories) — dataset category directory.
- [GET /v1/datasets/{source}/{dataset}](/v1/datasets) — dataset metadata.
- [GET /v1/datasets/{source}/{dataset}/records](/v1/datasets) — paginated records
  from cache (`?offset=&limit=`).
- [GET /v1/insights](/v1/insights) — AI-agent generated findings (`?limit=`).
- [GET /v1/insights/{id}/cite](/v1/insights) — reproducibility manifest for a
  finding (hash + the exact data window it was computed from).
- [GET /v1/insights/{id}/history](/v1/insights) — prior values of a finding over
  time (drift tracking).
- [GET /v1/brief](/v1/brief) — a short, human-readable roll-up of current
  findings.
- [GET /v1/silence-index](/v1/silence-index) — the roll-up score; scope with
  `?period=YYYY-Qn`.
- [GET /v1/alerts](/v1/alerts) — proactive alert dispatch log.
- [POST /v1/ask](/v1/ask) — natural-language Q&A over the data
  (body: `{"question": "..."}`).

## Optional links

- [Project README](https://github.com/alencheung/hkgov-rethink#readme)
- [Worked examples (verbatim output)](https://github.com/alencheung/hkgov-rethink/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md)
- [Architecture & determinism guarantee](https://github.com/alencheung/hkgov-rethink/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [Interactive dashboard](/dashboard)

## Notes for agents

- Prefer the JSON endpoints above for structured access; this index is for
  orientation only.
- `/v1/brief` is a good one-call summary to ground a response in current data.
- `/v1/insights/{id}/cite` exists specifically so a claim can be verified against
  the exact source window — use it before quoting a finding.
- This file is served at `/llms.txt` by both the Rust API binary and the static
  dashboard host; the content is identical.
